seoTitle: Starpower Solutions | EV Charging, Solar Inverter, Motor Drive | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Explore Starpower application solutions for EV charging stations, solar inverters, and industrial motor drives. Complete with BOM lists and technical support.

faqs.question: What solutions does Starpower offer for EV charging applications?
faqs.answer: Starpower offers comprehensive solutions for EV charging infrastructure: (1) DC fast charging modules using 1200V SiC MOSFETs for high-efficiency power conversion up to 350kW. (2) Onboard charger solutions with integrated power modules for AC charging up to 22kW. (3) Power factor correction (PFC) stages using IGBT modules for grid-friendly operation. (4) DC-DC converter modules for voltage level conversion. These solutions feature high efficiency (>98%), compact size, and bidirectional capability for vehicle-to-grid (V2G) applications. Starpower provides reference designs, BOM lists, and technical support for EV charging system development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EV charging system design support and component recommendations.

faqs.question: What are Starpower's solar inverter solutions?
faqs.answer: Starpower provides complete solutions for solar inverter applications: (1) String inverter solutions using 1200V IGBT modules for residential and commercial systems up to 150kW. (2) Central inverter solutions with high-power modules for utility-scale installations. (3) SiC-based solutions for high-frequency inverters with reduced passive component size. (4) Three-phase inverter configurations for grid-tied applications. These solutions offer high efficiency (>98.5%), wide MPPT voltage range, and reliable operation in harsh outdoor environments. Starpower modules are qualified for solar applications with extensive field proven reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for solar inverter design support and topology optimization.

faqs.question: How can Starpower modules be used in motor drive applications?
faqs.answer: Starpower modules are ideal for various motor drive applications: (1) Variable frequency drives (VFD) using 1200V IGBT modules for AC motor control up to 500kW. (2) Servo drive solutions with fast-switching modules for precision motion control. (3) Traction drives for electric vehicles using automotive-qualified modules. (4) Soft starter solutions using thyristor modules for reduced mechanical stress. These solutions provide smooth speed control, high torque accuracy, and energy savings through variable speed operation. Starpower offers application notes, reference designs, and thermal management guidelines for motor drive designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for motor drive system design and control algorithm support.

faqs.question: What support does BeiLuo provide for Starpower solution implementation?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo provides comprehensive support for Starpower solution implementation: (1) Technical consultation - helping select the right modules for your application requirements. (2) Reference designs - complete schematics, PCB layouts, and BOM lists for common applications. (3) Thermal design support - heatsink selection, thermal simulation, and cooling system recommendations. (4) Gate driver design - compatible driver ICs and protection circuit recommendations. (5) Testing and validation - support for prototype testing and performance optimization. (6) Supply chain management - ensuring reliable supply of modules and supporting components. Our FAE team has extensive experience with Starpower modules in various applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to discuss your application and get implementation support.

faqs.question: Are Starpower solutions suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Starpower offers automotive-qualified solutions: (1) AEC-Q101 qualified modules for powertrain and auxiliary systems. (2) EV powertrain solutions using 1200V modules for traction inverters up to 200kW. (3) Onboard charger and DC-DC converter solutions for EVs. (4) 48V mild hybrid solutions for belt-driven starter generators. (5) Thermal management solutions designed for automotive temperature ranges (-40°C to +150°C). Starpower modules undergo extensive automotive qualification testing including temperature cycling, power cycling, and mechanical shock. PPAP documentation is available for automotive customers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive qualification data and PPAP documentation.

faqs.question: What tools and resources are available for Starpower solution development?
faqs.answer: Starpower and BeiLuo provide various tools and resources for solution development: (1) Datasheets and application notes with detailed electrical and thermal characteristics. (2) SPICE models for circuit simulation and performance prediction. (3) Thermal simulation tools and Rth curves for heatsink design. (4) Reference designs with complete schematics and PCB layouts. (5) Evaluation kits for prototype development and testing. (6) Online technical support and FAQ database. (7) Training materials and webinars on power module applications. These resources help engineers quickly develop and optimize their designs using Starpower modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to access development tools and resources for your project.

solutions.name: EV Charging Power Solution
solutions.shortDescription: High-efficiency power modules for DC fast charging and onboard charging applications
solutions.description: Complete power solution for electric vehicle charging infrastructure using Starpower SiC and IGBT modules
solutions.fullDescription: This EV charging solution leverages Starpower's advanced power modules to deliver high-efficiency, compact, and reliable charging systems. The solution covers both DC fast charging stations (50kW-350kW) and onboard chargers (3.3kW-22kW). Using Starpower's 1200V SiC MOSFET modules enables switching frequencies up to 100kHz, dramatically reducing passive component size and cooling requirements. For cost-sensitive applications, 1200V IGBT modules provide an optimal balance of performance and cost. The solution includes PFC stages for grid compliance, isolated DC-DC converters for voltage transformation, and bidirectional capability for V2G applications. Complete with detailed BOM, thermal design guidelines, and reference schematics.
features: High efficiency: >98% peak efficiency with SiC modules

customerCases.solution: Used GDS100M12B1 SiC modules in 3-phase Vienna PFC + LLC topology
customerCases.results: Achieved 98.5% efficiency, 30% smaller size than IGBT solution, passed UL certification
customerCases.quote: Starpower SiC modules enabled us to achieve industry-leading efficiency while reducing system size and cost.

customerCases.solution: Implemented dual-active bridge using GDS50M12B1 modules

customerCases.quote: The automotive-qualified SiC modules from Starpower met all our reliability and performance requirements.

faeInsights.overview: As BeiLuo's FAE team, we've implemented numerous EV charging designs using Starpower modules. Here are key insights:
keyPoints: SiC modules enable 2-3x higher switching frequency than IGBT, reducing magnetics size by 40-50%
Gate drive design is critical - use isolated drivers with at least 5A peak current and negative turn-off voltage
Thermal management: Plan for 0.3-0.5K/W heatsink thermal resistance for 100A modules at full load
EMI filtering: High dv/dt requires careful filter design - plan for additional common-mode filtering
Parallel operation: SiC modules parallel better than IGBT due to positive temperature coefficient
Protection: Implement fast overcurrent protection (<2μs) due to limited short-circuit withstand time
recommendations: For 150kW+ chargers: Use GDS100M12B1 in 3-level topology for best efficiency
For onboard chargers: GDS50M12B1 offers optimal cost-performance balance
Always use Kelvin source connection for gate drive to minimize switching losses
Implement active Miller clamp for high dv/dt immunity
commonIssues: Gate oscillations: Use proper gate resistor damping (5-10Ω) and minimize loop inductance
Thermal runaway: Ensure adequate heatsinking and implement temperature monitoring
EMI issues: Implement proper shielding and filtering due to high switching speeds
faeInsights.author: FAE Team
faeInsights.content: This solution provides excellent performance for target applications.

faqs.question: What is the recommended topology for a 150kW DC fast charger using Starpower modules?
faqs.answer: For a 150kW DC fast charger, we recommend a two-stage topology: (1) Input stage - Three-phase Vienna PFC using GDS100M12B1 SiC modules. This provides unity power factor and 800V DC bus voltage with high efficiency. (2) DC-DC stage - Dual-active bridge (DAB) or LLC resonant converter using GDS100M12B1 for isolation and voltage regulation. This topology achieves >98% efficiency with SiC modules. Alternative: Use IGBT modules (GD100HFL120C2S) for cost-sensitive applications with slightly lower efficiency (~97%). The Vienna PFC + LLC combination offers the best balance of efficiency, power density, and cost for this power level.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed topology analysis and design optimization for your specific requirements.

faqs.question: How do I design the gate drive for Starpower SiC modules in EV chargers?
faqs.answer: Gate drive design for Starpower SiC modules requires careful attention: (1) Voltage levels: Use +18V turn-on and -3V to -5V turn-off. The negative voltage is critical for dv/dt immunity. (2) Gate resistance: 5-10Ω for turn-on and turn-off to control switching speed and EMI. (3) Peak current: Gate driver must provide 5-10A peak current for fast switching. (4) Isolation: Use reinforced isolation (5kV+) due to high dv/dt. (5) Protection: Implement active Miller clamp and desaturation detection. (6) Layout: Minimize gate loop inductance (<10nH) with proper PCB design. Recommended drivers: 1EDI20N12AF, UCC21520, or 2EDF7275K. Starpower provides reference gate drive circuits in application notes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate drive circuit design and component recommendations.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for Starpower modules in EV chargers?
faqs.answer: Thermal management for Starpower modules in EV chargers: (1) Heatsink selection: Target RthSA of 0.2-0.4K/W for 100A modules at full load. (2) Thermal interface: Use high-performance TIM with 0.1-0.2K·cm²/W thermal resistance. (3) Cooling method: Forced air cooling typically required; liquid cooling for high ambient temperatures. (4) Temperature monitoring: Use NTC thermistor integrated in module for over-temperature protection. (5) Thermal derating: Plan for 80% current rating at 60°C ambient. (6) Thermal simulation: Use Starpower's thermal models to verify design. Example: GDS100M12B1 at 100A produces ~120W loss; with RthJC=0.4K/W and RthSA=0.3K/W, Tj = Ta + 120W × (0.4+0.3+0.1) = 40°C + 96°C = 136°C (acceptable).
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection support.

faqs.question: How do I achieve bidirectional operation for V2G applications?
faqs.answer: Bidirectional operation for V2G using Starpower modules: (1) Topology: Use dual-active bridge (DAB) or CLLC resonant converter which are naturally bidirectional. (2) Control: Implement synchronous rectification on both sides with proper phase control. (3) Power flow: Control direction by adjusting phase shift between primary and secondary bridges. (4) Efficiency: Bidirectional operation maintains >97% efficiency in both directions with SiC modules. (5) Protection: Implement current limiting in both directions and DC bus voltage monitoring. (6) Grid synchronization: Ensure proper grid connection and anti-islanding protection for G2V operation. Starpower modules' fast switching enables efficient bidirectional power flow with minimal additional components.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for bidirectional converter design and control strategy.

solutions.name: Solar Inverter Power Solution
solutions.shortDescription: High-efficiency IGBT and SiC modules for residential, commercial, and utility-scale solar inverters
solutions.description: Complete power solution for solar photovoltaic inverters using Starpower IGBT and SiC modules
solutions.fullDescription: This solar inverter solution utilizes Starpower's proven power modules to deliver high-efficiency, reliable energy conversion for PV systems. The solution covers string inverters (3kW-150kW) for residential and commercial installations, as well as central inverters for utility-scale plants. Using Starpower's 1200V IGBT modules (GD50HFL120C1S, GD100HFL120C2S) provides cost-effective solutions with >98% efficiency. For next-generation designs, SiC modules (GDS100M12B1) enable switching frequencies up to 50kHz, reducing passive component size by 30-40%. The solution includes MPPT boost stages, three-phase inverter bridges, and grid-tie filters. Complete with detailed BOM, thermal design guidelines, and compliance recommendations for grid codes.

faeInsights.overview: Based on our experience with solar inverter designs, here are key insights for using Starpower modules:
keyPoints: T-type three-level topology offers best efficiency (>98.5%) for 1000V DC systems
Two-level topology with SiC modules is cost-effective for 1500V DC systems
Switching frequency trade-off: 16kHz for IGBT, 32-50kHz for SiC
Thermal design critical: Plan for 60°C ambient with full solar irradiance
MPPT boost stage: Use interleaved design for >50kW to reduce current ripple
Grid filter: LCL filter design must consider switching frequency and grid impedance
recommendations: For 50kW+ inverters: Consider three-level topology for best efficiency
Use SiC modules for new designs to achieve highest power density
Implement proper derating for 60°C ambient operation
Plan for 25-year life with conservative thermal design
commonIssues: Ground leakage current: Use proper EMI filtering and shielding
PID degradation: Implement proper grounding for module frames
Hot spots: Ensure uniform cooling across all power modules
faeInsights.author: FAE Team
faeInsights.content: This solution provides excellent performance for target applications.

faqs.question: What topology is recommended for a 100kW solar inverter using Starpower modules?
faqs.answer: For a 100kW solar inverter, we recommend a T-type three-level NPC topology using GD100HFL120C2S IGBT modules: (1) DC-AC stage: T-type three-level inverter with 6x GD100HFL120C2S (2 per phase). This topology offers >98.5% efficiency with 1000V DC input. (2) MPPT stage: Interleaved boost using 2x GD50HFL120C1S for wide voltage range. (3) Grid filter: LCL filter designed for 16kHz switching frequency. Alternative: Use two-level topology with SiC modules (GDS100M12B1) at 32kHz for higher power density but slightly higher cost. The T-type NPC provides best efficiency and EMI performance for this power level with IGBT modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for topology selection and inverter design optimization.

faqs.question: How do I select between IGBT and SiC modules for solar inverters?
faqs.answer: Selection between IGBT and SiC modules for solar inverters depends on priorities: (1) Cost-sensitive applications: Use IGBT (GD100HFL120C2S) with 16kHz switching. Achieves >98% efficiency at lower cost. (2) Premium/high-density applications: Use SiC (GDS100M12B1) with 32-50kHz switching. Achieves >98.5% efficiency with 30-40% smaller magnetics. (3) Hybrid approach: Use SiC for MPPT boost (high-frequency) and IGBT for inverter (lower frequency) for cost-performance balance. (4) 1500V systems: SiC preferred due to lower switching losses at higher voltage. ROI analysis: SiC adds ~15-20% module cost but reduces system cost by 10-15% through smaller passive components and cooling. For 25-year life, SiC's higher efficiency provides additional energy revenue.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for cost-benefit analysis and module selection guidance.

faqs.question: What thermal design considerations are important for solar inverters?
faqs.answer: Thermal design for solar inverters with Starpower modules: (1) Ambient temperature: Design for 60°C worst-case with full solar irradiance. (2) Heatsink design: Target RthSA of 0.15-0.25K/W for 100A modules at full load in 60°C ambient. (3) Cooling: Forced air cooling with 200-400CFM airflow typically required; natural convection only for low power (<10kW). (4) Thermal interface: High-performance TIM essential for reliable heat transfer over 25-year life. (5) Temperature derating: Plan for 80% current at 60°C ambient. (6) Hot spot management: Ensure uniform cooling across all modules; avoid temperature gradients >10°C between modules. (7) Thermal monitoring: Use module NTC for over-temperature protection at 100°C heatsink temperature. Example calculation: GDS100M12B1 at 100A, 60°C ambient, RthSA=0.2K/W: Tj = 60 + 120W × (0.4+0.2+0.1) = 60 + 84 = 144°C (acceptable with margin).
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and cooling system design.

faqs.question: How do I design the MPPT boost stage for wide voltage range?
faqs.answer: MPPT boost stage design for wide voltage range (200-1000V) using Starpower modules: (1) Topology: Interleaved boost converter with 2 phases for >50kW to reduce current ripple. (2) Module selection: GD50HFL120C1S (50A) for boost switch; GDR200A12 (200A) for boost diode or use synchronous rectification with second IGBT. (3) Inductor design: Calculate for 20-30% current ripple at minimum input voltage. For 100kW, 200V input: L = V × dt / dI = 200V × 25μs / 15A = 333μH. (4) Switching frequency: 16kHz for IGBT, 50kHz for SiC. (5) MPPT algorithm: Perturb and observe with 0.5% voltage step for stable tracking. (6) Efficiency: Target >99% for boost stage alone. (7) Protection: Input overvoltage (1100V), overcurrent (150% rated), and reverse polarity protection. Starpower modules' low switching losses enable high-frequency operation for compact boost designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for MPPT boost design and component selection.
